name: BoardGameGeek MCP Server
what_it_does: Community MCP server providing access to the BoardGameGeek (BGG) API through MCP. Enables AI agents to retrieve board game information — game ratings, reviews, player counts, mechanics, categories, and community data from BGG's comprehensive board game database. Useful for game recommendation, collection management, and board game research agents.
use_cases: Board game recommendation agents querying BGG ratings and reviews
Collection management agents tracking board game inventory and wishlists
Research agents analyzing board game mechanics, categories, and player counts
Gaming event planning agents finding games by player count and play time
not_for: Video game or digital game databases (board/tabletop game focus)
Real-time game session tracking (catalog and community data focus)
Commercial board game retail (ratings/info only, not pricing)
best_when: A board game enthusiast's AI assistant or recommendation agent needs to query BGG's comprehensive board game catalog — finding games by mechanics, player count, rating, or category.
avoid_when: You need video game data (use IGDB for that). BGG is exclusively for tabletop/board games.

auth.notes: No authentication required — BGG API is public for read access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — BGG API is open and free
pricing.notes: BGG XML API2 is free and public. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: BGG API has informal rate limits — be respectful with request frequency

known_agent_gotchas: BGG XML API2 uses XML responses — verify the MCP parses these correctly for your use case
BGG API can be slow during peak hours — implement caching for frequently accessed games
BGG has informal rate limits — space requests to avoid temporary blocks
Community server (kkjdaniel) — verify game search and collection retrieval features
agent_readiness.security_notes: Public board game data — non-sensitive. No auth. HTTPS. Low security risk for typical game recommendation use.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public read-only board game data. No auth. Low risk.
